audit.scope: priority fleet (L1 audit set) — 19 repos in scope
audit.selection_rule: ci.yml with 0 timeout-minutes lines AND a defined jobs: section

jobs.name: Repository hygiene
jobs.rationale: lint-class: pure metadata validation (cargo metadata, bun install) — 10 min ample

jobs.name: CI (reusable)
jobs.rationale: default CI: calls KooshaPari/phenotype-infrakit reusable ci.yaml — 30 min covers cargo build+test+clippy

jobs.name: CI (reusable rust-ci)
jobs.rationale: default CI: calls rust-ci reusable (placeholder SHA; rust version 1.75) — 30 min default

jobs.name: Test
jobs.rationale: default CI: cargo fmt+check+clippy+test+release build — 30 min default

jobs.name: Miri
jobs.rationale: integration-class: nightly miri UB detector; known to be slow — 60 min

jobs.name: Quality gates
jobs.rationale: lint-class: ruff + pyright on small Python src — 10 min

jobs.name: Package metadata
jobs.rationale: lint-class: bash -n on 4 packaging scripts + 3 dry-run validators — 10 min

jobs.name: Unit tests
jobs.rationale: default CI: pytest tests/unit — 20 min (mid-default, smaller suite)

jobs.name: Backend smoke
jobs.rationale: default CI: install.sh + serve + curl health-poll loop + smoke.sh — 30 min default

next_steps: User reviews branches and (optionally) opens PRs to merge into main
After merge, sweep 10 remaining priority repos (Tracely, phenoRuntime, phenoShared, FocalPoint, HeliosCLI, PhenoProc, pheno, HexaKit, Pyron, agentapi-plusplus) per the same pattern

Then sweep active+new repos (AppGen, AuthKit, Benchora, BytePort, Civis, Conft, DataKit, DevHex, Eidolon, Eventra, plus Authvault, phenotype-landing, phenotype-gates, phenotype-runs, phenotype-org-governance)
